summary: McKenzie asks his blog readers for help debugging his Mac version of Bingo Card Creator after receiving bug reports from VersionTracker.com users. Despite having some successful Mac users, two users report the app dies on startup due to Java being unable to find the main class. He suspects an issue with the Mac-specific pinfo.list file but cannot reproduce or diagnose it since he has no Mac experience. The post is a straightforward request for community testing assistance.
key_points: VersionTracker.com listing generated multiple sales within 12 hours, demonstrating the value of software distribution sites for indie developers
Cross-platform Java deployment creates platform-specific debugging challenges, especially when the developer lacks access to the target platform
McKenzie leverages his blog audience as a free QA resource, a practical tactic for solo software developers with limited hardware access
money_quotes: I've already got multiple sales (!) and also multiple bug reports that it doesn't work on various versions of Mac OS X.
These numbers might as well be Greek to me since I have no experience using Macs.
This is behavior I am unfortunately distressingly familiar with on Windows, and it is generally the result of a faulty manifest file.
key_insight: Solo software developers can compensate for limited resources by leveraging their blog audience for cross-platform QA testing they cannot do themselves.
